articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Denmark beat Norway 3-1 in final Euro warm-up
article.description: Denmark wrapped up their Euro 2024 preparations with a 3-1 win over Norway at Brondby Stadium that saw Pierre-Emile Hojbjerg score for the third international game in a row, with Erling Haaland grabbing a consolation goal for the visitors.
article.linkText: Denmark beat Norway in final Euros warm-up
article.story:
Denmark wrapped up their Euro 2024 preparations with a 3-1 win over Norway at Brondby Stadium that saw Pierre-Emile Hojbjerg score for the third international game in a row, with Erling Haaland grabbing a consolation goal for the visitors.

Midfielder Hojbjerg got Saturday's proceedings underway with a 12th-minute strike from his powerful right foot, and the Danes added a second when defender Jannick Vestergaard headed home Andreas Skov Olsen's corner nine minutes later.

With Denmark making a slew of second-half changes, Haaland had a goal chalked off after a VAR review in the 65th minute but there was no stopping him seven minutes later when a superb Martin Odegaard pass teed him up to reduce the deficit.

Youssef Poulsen netted a third in second-half stoppage time to give the Danes the perfect send-off as they head to Germany, where they will face Slovenia, England and Serbia in Group C, with the Slovenians up first in Stuttgart on June 16.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Denmark 1, Norway 0. Pierre-Emile Højbjerg (Denmark) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner.
Leo Østigård (Norway) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Denmark 2, Norway 0. Jannik Vestergaard (Denmark) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Andreas Skov Olsen with a cross   following a corner.
Substitution, Norway. Aron Dønnum replaces Antonio Nusa.
Substitution, Norway. David Møller Wolfe replaces Marcus Pedersen.
Substitution, Denmark. Thomas Delaney replaces Morten Hjulmand.
Pierre-Emile Højbjerg (Denmark) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Denmark. Simon Kjær replaces Andreas Christensen.
Substitution, Denmark. Joakim Mæhle replaces Alexander Bah.
Substitution, Denmark. Kasper Dolberg replaces Rasmus Højlund.
Substitution, Denmark. Christian Eriksen replaces Andreas Skov Olsen.
Goal!  Denmark 2, Norway 1. Erling Haaland (Norway) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Martin Ødegaard with a through ball.
Substitution, Norway. Andreas Hanche-Olsen replaces Julian Ryerson because of an injury.
Substitution, Norway. Patrick Berg replaces Morten Thorsby.
Substitution, Denmark. Yussuf Poulsen replaces Mikkel Damsgaard.
Substitution, Norway. Erik Botheim replaces Oscar Bobb.
Substitution, Norway. Hugo Vetlesen replaces Sander Berge.
Goal!  Denmark 3, Norway 1. Yussuf Poulsen (Denmark) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Victor Kristiansen.
Second Half ends, Denmark 3, Norway 1.
